items.name: authorize
items.description: Authorization helpers — role-based access control (RBAC), permission-based access, and policy classes. Works with any authentication middleware that sets `req.user`.

methods.description: Role-based authorization middleware. Checks `req.user.role` or `req.user.roles` against allowed roles. Returns 401 if `req.user` is missing (not authenticated). Returns 403 if the user's role is not in the allowed list.

examples.title: Multiple Roles

methods.description: Permission-based authorization middleware. Checks `req.user.permissions` (array or Set) for the required permission(s). Permission strings follow a `resource:action` convention: - `'posts:write'` — write access to posts - `'users:delete'` — delete users - `'*'` — superuser wildcard

methodParams.notes: Required permissions (ALL must be present unless `opts.any` is true).

examples.title: Require ALL Permissions
Require ANY Permission

methods.description: Like `can()`, but passes if the user has ANY of the listed permissions.

methodParams.notes: Permissions to check (any one is sufficient).

methods.description: Check if an action is allowed. Falls through to the action method if defined, otherwise denies.
Policy gate middleware. Runs a policy check against a resource loaded from the request.

methodGroups.group: req.user helpers (mixed in by middleware barrel)

methods.description: Attach convenience authorization methods to `req.user`. Call this middleware after JWT/session middleware. Adds: - `req.user.is(...roles)` — check roles - `req.user.can(...perms)` — check permissions
